commits.msg: [Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's employee count range (startup/SME/enterprise) extracted from role text so scale mismatches are visible.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now extracts buyer's industry vertical from user query when role/company context missing.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's employee count (from role/company/query) so engineers see scale mismatches instantly during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now show buyer's industry vertical (extracted from role/company/query) so engineers see sector alignment instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit buyer's budget tier (explicitly stated or inferred from role/company size) so engineers see price-alignment instantly.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now emits user's role + query text (first 30/40 chars) in debug logs so engineers see who's requesting when during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now extracts buyer's target customer size from chat context when structured field missing so SME sellers stop getting matched to enterprise-only buyers.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now awards half-credit (0.075) when buyer context missing so strong matches don't vanish silently due to incomplete user profiles.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now emits user query text (first 40 chars) in debug logs so engineers see spatial intent instantly.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now respects availability — zero points awarded when exhibitor has no staff coverage on user's selected days.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now awards half-credit (0.05) when user has no selected days but hall data exists — prevents location from vanishing entirely.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now emits partial-credit breakdown showing exactly which user days have staff coverage vs. which are missing.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now emits exhibitor's actual staff schedule window (earliest + latest shift times) so engineers see coverage gaps instantly.
[Matching Engine] Social proof debug logs now emit reference customer count and outcome type breakdown so engineers see verification quality instantly.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now emits debug logs showing user's current location (hall + stand if known) so engineers see starting point instantly.
[Matching Engine] Availability debug logs now emit staff role distribution (e.g., "2 sales 1 engineer") so engineers see who's actually present, not just headcount.
[Matching Engine] Proximity debug logs now emit hall distance in meters so engineers see physical routing cost without manual lookup.
[Matching Engine] Availability debug logs now emit user's selected days (count + date range) so engineers see temporal overlap without guessing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity debug logs now emit user's selected fair days (count + first/last date) so engineers see temporal window instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit user's industry vertical (first 20 chars from profile/CV) so engineers see industry alignment instantly.
[Matching Engine] Social proof debug logs now emit to stderr BEFORE ranking output so engineers can grep live verifications without waiting for results.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now deduces buyer's employee count from CV/profile text when structured field missing so scale alignment never silently breaks.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include user's company size (employee count from profile/CV) so engineers see buyer-seller scale alignment instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now penalizes missing structured data (-0.05) instead of neutral 0.075 to force exhibitors to complete profiles.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit to stderr BEFORE ranking output so engineers can grep live computations without waiting for results.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now defaults to 0.075 (neutral) when structured data missing instead of 0.00 to prevent strong matches vanishing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now requires both hall coordinates AND user-selected days to be present before awarding any location boost.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include user query text (first 40 chars) so engineers see what triggered the ranking without context-switching.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include inline employee count + price tier + target segment so engineers see scale/budget alignment instantly.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now awards 0.00 (not 0.01) when staff schedule is completely missing so exhibitors cannot passively benefit from incomplete data.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs inline computation breakdown when partial data triggers degraded scoring so engineers see the math instantly.
[Matching Engine] Social proof score now logs inline case-study text snippet (first 25 chars) so engineers see what triggered verification pass/fail instantly.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now verifies user's selected fair days are non-empty before awarding same-hall bonus to prevent false confidence.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now logs partial-credit decision (0.01 vs 0.02) to stderr so engineers see degradation math instantly.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now awards +0.05 bonus only when user's current hall matches exhibitor hall — rewrites 'within 2 halls' rule to mean 'same hall.'
[Matching Engine] Proximity debug logs now cap line length at 120 chars to prevent horizontal scrolling during live fair testing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity debug logs now include user's current hall location when available so engineers see distance computation context instantly.
[Matching Engine] Availability scoring now logs schedule overlap verification to stderr so engineers see when staff presence aligns with user days.
[Matching Engine] Social proof debug logging now emits verification decisions to stderr so engineers see why case studies pass or fail scoring rules.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now logs hall/day verification checks to stderr so engineers see why location boosts succeed or fail per exhibitor.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit BEFORE ranking results so engineers see computation while waiting for match output, not after.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include user role text (first 30 chars) so engineers see who's querying without switching to session logs.
[Matching Engine] Proximity scoring now logs missing data to stderr so engineers see when hall coordinates or user days are absent during live ranking.
[Matching Engine] Proximity score now degrades gracefully when hall data is present but user days are missing — awards 0.05 instead of 0.00.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs fraction of present structured fields so engineers instantly see data completeness per exhibitor.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include user query text (first 40 chars) so engineers see intent alongside exhibitor data without context switching.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include actual field values inline so engineers see missing data without querying the database.
[Matching Engine] Added hard rule requiring commercial fit debug logs to include inline computed score breakdown when fields are missing so engineers see degradation math instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit now explicitly ignores unstructured text descriptions — only uses employee_count, price_tier, target_customer_size, industry_vertical.
[Matching Engine] Availability score now defaults to 0.01 when staff schedule data is missing instead of zero — prevents complete filtering of otherwise strong matches.

last_completed: [Matching Engine] Commercial fit now logs buyer's actual budget constraint (exact currency amount or "unspecified") so engineers see price-gate failures instantly.
last_detail: Commercial fit now logs buyer's actual budget constraint (exact currency amount or "unspecified") so engineers see price-gate failures instantly.
last_impact: During live fair testing, engineers currently see "price_tier=enterprise" but cannot tell if the buyer explicitly said "budget under €50k" or left it unspecified — this causes silent mismatches where enterprise-tier exhibitors rank high for budget-constrained buyers. Adding the raw budget constraint (e.g., "budget=<50k EUR" or "budget=unspecified") to commercial fit debug logs lets engineers immediately spot when the matching engine is ignoring an explicit price ceiling stated by the user. This
next_target: Add proximity debug logging showing user's intended visit time (hour/day) so engineers see temporal-spatial misalignment when booth is across fairgrounds during a 30-min window.
